error_signature: starting_after not nested in pagination object
title: Intercom pagination fails due to incorrect JSON structure
symptom: The HTTP Request node fails to send the 'starting_after' cursor in the correct nested structure, leading to pagination issues. Responses loop with identical data instead of fetching new pages.
not_this_if: Pagination works correctly with manual JSON body
Using a different API that does not require nested pagination
root_cause: The HTTP Request node in n8n does not correctly format the JSON body for APIs that require specific nesting of parameters. It places 'starting_after' at the root level instead of within the 'pagination' object, causing the API to reject the request.
fix_steps: Create a workflow with two HTTP Request nodes.
In the first node, send the request without 'starting_after'.
In the second node, set the body to include 'starting_after' nested within the 'pagination' object, using the expression '{{ $json.pages.next.starting_after }}'.
Add a conditional check to see if 'next.starting_after' exists before proceeding.
agent_instruction: Set up two HTTP Request nodes to handle pagination correctly by nesting 'starting_after' in the second node's body.
